authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 123 - Fish and Fishery Products
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal production and handling rules for fishery products, used to identify what counts as sufficient processing and handling evidence.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines when processing and handling claims can be advanced based on objective records.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative U.S. rule set for food-traceability record retention and chain responsibilities across covered supply flows.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents completion without lot/chain records that survive cross-system mismatch checks.

authoritative_sources.title: 50 CFR Part 21 - Fishery Management
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal fishery-management context establishes catch and timing boundaries that constrain execution decisions.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Enables explicit seasonality and jurisdiction checks before lifecycle advancement.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA NRCS Soil and Water Conservation Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operationally authoritative resource for land, water, and stewardship conditions that can alter field or pond operations.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Anchors decisions that must pause when seasonal field conditions materially change.

authoritative_sources.title: EPA Agriculture and environmental compliance program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides federal environmental constraints and interpretation context for runoff, contamination, and operational risk escalation.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Triggers environmental escalation when completion and transfer posture conflict with external risk context.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 EPCIS
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies or vendor-neutral workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral framework for event, lot, and transfer traceability semantics across systems.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Enables consistent fisheries traceability terms and prevents generic assumptions under mismatched identifiers.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA National Organic Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Additional production-control overlay for organic fisheries/land-linked workflows where this tenant context is active.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Ensures tenant program context changes evidence requirements rather than collapsing to generic completion.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and dependency verification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, jurisdictional rules, tenant policy context, case identifiers, and required records before processing a lifecycle action.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle-state assessment
workflow_stages.description: Map request state to accepted, blocked, rework, hold, escalate, or complete based on evidence and owner context.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and evidence reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize lot, catch, transfer, and land-field records; identify missing or conflicting lineage and route escalation when unresolved.
source_classes: Recognized standards bodies or vendor-neutral workflow references
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Seasonal and environmental gating
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate weather, season, land, and environmental conditions before completion or handoff decisions.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Closure and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Emit completion evidence only when blockers are closed; escalate legal/permit/veterinary/environmental/food-safety requests when authority boundary is crossed.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

artifacts: vessel logbook or aquaculture lot logs
land, farm, and water-body context records
traceability chain: lot IDs, batch IDs, movement tickets

allowed: Validate and progress lifecycle states when required evidence and source context are present.
Prepare blocked, exception, rework, and completion outputs tied to named records and handoff prerequisites.
Escalate when conditions materially change due to seasonality, contamination, or policy conflict.
escalate: Food-safety or environmental interpretation changes execution posture.
Jurisdictional fishery timing, permit, or traceability constraints are contested.
Source conflicts materially alter lifecycle progression and require human resolution.
refuse: Requests for legal, veterinary, environmental permit, or financial-approval authority.
Requests for completion or closure without full traceability or systems-of-record evidence.
Requests to provide safety-critical treatment or policy-override decisions outside this lane.

domain_failure_modes: Marking completion before traceability, environmental, and ownership evidence is complete.
Ignoring seasonal/field conditions that invalidate normal handling windows.
Proceeding after catch/lot IDs diverge between systems without escalation.
Crossing into agronomic, veterinary, environmental authority signoff without routing.
Applying generic execution assumptions across jurisdictions with different fishery rules.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and regional fisheries authority nuances
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Regional closures, permit conditions, and species-specific controls vary by jurisdiction; handled via required tenant context and routed escalation when missing.
When log, land, and traceability systems disagree, the lane escalates and blocks completion rather than selecting authority silently.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Production cycle definition for mixed aquaculture and wild-catch workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Resolution is deferred to tenant-defined cycle definitions and production policy retrieved at runtime.

notes: Source set includes federal regulatory, environmental, and vendor-neutral standards to cover workflow, evidence, and boundary behavior.
All required domain scenario families are covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity is retained; runtime is required to capture missing jurisdiction, system, and policy context before progression.
